data.description:
## Goal
Make the `code_health` metric lens portable to non-Python client projects by replacing rebar-specific, Python-only readers with a configurable, per-project **code-analyzer** built on actively-maintained polyglot OSS tools. Ship the analyzer + its tool dependencies as an **optional `[metrics]` extra** so rebar core gains no new hard dependencies.

## Why
Audit finding (portability review): of 15 registered metrics, only the `code_health` lens is truly language-coupled. `module_size_distribution` / `cap_change_events` / module-size seeds hardcode `src/rebar/**/*.py`, LOC-of-.py, and `.github/module-size-limit.txt` (a rebar governance artifact). Used as-is by a client they DON'T fail loudly — they either fabricate authoritative-labeled zeros (scan an absent path -> {count:0,max_loc:0} at confidence=high) or leak a raw FileNotFoundError as the 'unavailable' reason. Ticket-derived (agent_process) and git-delta (churn/refactor_ratio) metrics are already language-agnostic and stay in core.

## Recommended default stack (deterministic, no network, permissive licenses, verified actively-maintained 2026)
- **scc** (MIT, Go) — LOC + fast size/DRYness + approx complexity + COCOMO. v4.x, 8.5k stars.
- **lizard** (MIT, Python) — TRUE cyclomatic complexity (CCN) + token/param counts across ~25 languages. v1.23 (2026). Already a rebar dev/backfill dep.
- **jscpd** (MIT) — duplication/clone detection, 223 formats, Rabin-Karp. v5. Already a rebar dep.
- (rust-code-analysis has richer metrics — cognitive/Halstead/MI — but is STALE, last release 2023; not adopted.)

## Design
- Define an analyzer interface: `analyze(repo_root, languages) -> {loc, complexity, duplication}`, shelling out to the configured tool(s); deterministic, no LLM.
- Per-project config selects tool + thresholds; the rebar module-size CAP becomes one optional policy layered on the generic LOC signal (not hardcoded).
- **Hard fallback contract:** if no analyzer is configured for the client's language, emit a genuine `Unavailable("no analyzer configured for <lang>")` — NEVER scan a hardcoded path, NEVER fabricate zeros, NEVER leak an errno as a reason. (Fixes the silent-misleading-data mode found in the audit.)
- Package tools + analyzer under an optional `[metrics]` extra; core install stays dependency-free and degrades to Unavailable when the extra is absent.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `code_health` metrics computed via the analyzer stack (scc/lizard/jscpd) for a Python client AND at least one non-Python client (e.g. Go or TS).
- [ ] Zero new HARD deps in rebar core; analyzer + tools live behind `pip install rebar[metrics]`; core-only install reports code_health as Unavailable with a clear reason.
- [ ] No hardcoded `src/rebar` / `.github/module-size-limit.txt`; scan root + size policy are per-project config.
- [ ] Missing analyzer/tool -> honest `Unavailable`, never fabricated zeros and never a leaked exception string.
- [ ] Module-size cap re-expressed as an optional policy over the LOC signal.
- [ ] Docs (reuse-surface, user-guide) describe the extra, config, and the fallback contract.

## Out of scope
- Polyglot **dependency-graph** analysis — tracked separately as an idea (no mature drop-in polyglot tool exists; needs per-language adapters or tree-sitter/stack-graphs/SCIP). Explicitly NOT part of this epic.
data.title: Code-health metrics: adopt polyglot analyzer stack (scc/lizard/jscpd) behind a [metrics] extra
